_comment: Source: NASEMSO National Model EMS Clinical Guidelines v3.0 (March 2022). Toxins and Environmental: Drowning. NASEMSO national provisional base.
level_display: All Levels — Interventions Scoped Per Provider Level
protocol_reference: NASEMSO National Model EMS Clinical Guidelines v3.0 — Toxins and Environmental: Drowning

sections.title: Patient Care Goals
sections.reference: NASEMSO National Model EMS Clinical Guidelines v3.0 — Toxins and Environmental: Drowning
points: Rapid assessment and management of life-threatening injuries
Rescue from the water-based environment
Transport patients suffering from drowning for hospital evaluation unless field arrest resuscitation termination guidelines apply.

sections.title: Patient Presentation
points: Patients suffering from drowning or drowning events independent of presence or absence of symptoms
EXCLUSION CRITERIA:
When protocol is inapplicable.

sections.title: EMR Interventions
sections.reference: NASEMSO v3.0 — EMR level (Emergency Medical Responder)
points: Rescue: rescuer safety first — use throw device or reach pole before entering water
CPR immediately on removal from water (do not delay for water removal from lungs — Heimlich not recommended for drowning)
AED as soon as available; dry patient before applying pads
BVM ventilation with high-flow O2
C-spine immobilization only if traumatic mechanism (diving, surfing, motor vehicle) — do not delay CPR

sections.title: EMT Interventions
sections.reference: NASEMSO v3.0 — EMT level (Emergency Medical Technician)
points: Aggressive airway management: suctioning, NPA/supraglottic airway
CPAP 5-10 cmH2O for pulmonary edema from submersion
12-lead ECG: dysrhythmias common
Blood glucose monitoring
Active rewarming for hypothermia component

sections.title: AEMT Interventions
points: IV/IO access: IV NS bolus for hypotension
Waveform capnography
Blood glucose monitoring and correction
Benzodiazepine for seizures
IO access if IV unsuccessful

sections.title: Paramedic Interventions
points: Endotracheal intubation and mechanical ventilation for persistent respiratory failure after CPAP
PEEP titration for pulmonary edema (5-10 cmH2O starting point)
Treat hypothermia per hypothermia protocol — common co-condition
Treat dysrhythmias per specific protocol
Vasopressors for refractory hemodynamic compromise
RSI: succinylcholine or rocuronium

sections.title: Patient Safety Considerations
points: Avoidance of hyperoxygenation of the drowning victim
Rescuer safety considerations

sections.title: Educational Pearls
points: The World Health Organization definition of drowning is " the process of experiencing respiratory impairment from submersion/immersion in liquid"
Drowning is further defined in the following categories: a. Non-fatal drowning: patients rescued from drowning
Submersion refers to situations in which the patient's airway is underwater. Immersion refers to situations in which the patient's body is in water, but the patient's airway remains out of the water
Pediatric Considerations: a. Drowning is a common cause of death in children
Rescue efforts should be coordinated between all responding agencies to ensure patient is rapidly accessed and removed from the water
Initiation of in-water ventilations may increase survival. In-water chest compressions are futile

The European Resuscitation Council recommends five initial breaths be provided to the drowning victim a. The initial ventilations may be more difficult to achieve as water in the airways may impede alveolar expansion
Active efforts to expel water from the airway (by abdominal thrusts or other means) should be avoided as they delay resuscitative efforts and increase the potential for vomiting and aspiration

sections.title: Key Documentation
points: ' Mechanism of injury or history suggesting cervical spine injury
' Submersion time
' Water temperature
' Activities leading to drowning
' Consider a standardized data collection metrics such as the Utstein drowning data reporting elements

EMR: Scene safety, primary survey, CPR, AED, BVM, bleeding control, oral airway, O2 therapy, epinephrine auto-injector for anaphylaxis, naloxone unit-dose auto-injector
EMT: EMR scope PLUS: NPA, supraglottic airway, CPAP, pulse oximetry, 12-lead ECG acquisition, oral glucose, aspirin, patient own nitroglycerin, albuterol SVN, IM epinephrine, blood glucose monitoring, cervical collar, traction splint
AEMT: EMT scope PLUS: waveform capnography, IO access, IV access initiation, IV/IO fluids, IV medications (dextrose, naloxone, epinephrine, glucagon, analgesics, antiemetics), subcutaneous/intranasal medications, nitroglycerin from stock
Paramedic: AEMT scope PLUS: ET intubation, RSI, cricothyrotomy, needle decompression, manual defibrillation, cardioversion, transcutaneous pacing, 12-lead ECG interpretation, all antidysrhythmics, vasopressors, all medication routes and classes

deterioration_flags: Contact medical direction for clinical deterioration or interventions outside standard scope
scope_notes: EMR scope is the minimum national standard — state/MCA may expand
All interventions subject to local medical director credentialing
Pediatric patients: use weight-based dosing and age-appropriate equipment
Contact medical direction when required by local protocol or for interventions outside standard scope
